title: From every stormy wind that blows
firstLine: From every stormy wind that blows,
publicDomainBasis: Hugh Stowell (1799–1865), Melody by Thomas Hastings (1784–1872). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
From every stormy wind that blows,
From every swelling tide of woes,
There is a calm, a sure retreat—
’Tis found beneath the mercy seat.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
There is a place where Jesus sheds
The oil of gladness on our heads,
A place than all besides more sweet—
It is the blood-bought mercy seat.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
There is a scene where spirits blend,
Where friend holds fellowship with friend;
Though sundered far, by faith they meet
Around one common mercy seat.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
There, there on eagle’s wings we soar,
And time and sense seem all no more,
And heaven comes down our souls to greet,
And glory crowns the mercy seat.
